name: Palencian Romanesque Natural Trail: Coking Coal Route
name_original: La Ruta de las Ermitas — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: This moderate 30.5-mile mountain biking route through Parque Natural Montaña Palentina offers varied terrain and wild landscapes.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Dehesa de Montejo offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, situated between the Cantabrian Mountains and the Meseta in Palencia, Spain. The area features an extensive plain of detrital plateaus intersected by rivers, creating varied terrain with gentle stretches and steeper inclines. Cyclists can navigate through rich woodlands of holm oaks, junipers, and beech trees, alongside lush riverine ecosystems. This region provides a mix of flat sections, rolling hills, and challenging ascents and descents, catering to various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Dehesa De Montejo The most popular mountain bike trail is Senda del Oso, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete, offering moderate climbs through varied natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta de la Embalsa de la Ruesga — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a moderate 12.0 miles (19.4 km) path that winds around the reservoir, providing scenic views of the natural park. Local mountain bikers also love the Palencian Romanesque Natural Trail: Coking Coal Route, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through historical landscapes and woodlands, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Dehesa de Montejo is defined by detrital plateaus, riverine ecosystems, and ancient woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle paths along riverbanks to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Dehesa de Montejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Dehesa de Montejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How challenging are the mountain bike trails in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Dehesa de Montejo offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. You'll find a mix of flat sections along riverbanks, rolling hills through cultivated fields, and more challenging steep ascents and descents. Out of 46 available routes, 4 are considered easy, 33 are moderate, and 9 are difficult, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Yes, Dehesa de Montejo has several trails perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature gentler inclines and less technical terrain, often following riverbanks or through more open landscapes. There are 4 easy routes available, providing a great introduction to mountain biking in the region.

Q: What options are there for experienced mountain bikers in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging options in Dehesa de Montejo. The region's diverse topography includes routes with significant elevation gain and technical sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Triollo Village – La Bárcena Watermill loop from Cervera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.8 km route with over 1200 meters of elevation gain, offering a strenuous and rewarding ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the easier routes in Dehesa de Montejo are generally suitable for families. These trails often follow less demanding terrain, such as paths along river courses or through gentle woodlands, providing a pleasant experience for all ages. Look for routes marked as 'easy' for the most suitable options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Many of the natural trails in Dehesa de Montejo are suitable for dogs, especially those that traverse woodlands and open fields. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. The region's extensive woodlands and riverine ecosystems offer pleasant environments for rides with your canine companion.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trails in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around the villages and trailheads in Dehesa de Montejo. Many routes start from accessible points where you can find designated parking areas or roadside parking. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking recommendations.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Dehesa de Montejo is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, lush landscapes, and less extreme weather conditions. Summer can be enjoyable, especially in the shaded woodland areas, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter riding is possible, but some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Dehesa de Montejo is rich in natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can explore diverse woodlands of holm oaks, junipers, and ancient yew trees, particularly in areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejeda de Tosande Yew Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer panoramic views of the Cantabrian Mountains and the Meseta. You might also encounter riverine ecosystems, detrital plateaus, and even unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4814279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fossil Forest Cliff&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Yes, Dehesa de Montejo offers several circular mountain bike routes, which are great for exploring the region without needing a return trip. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1178282721/hike-from-reoba-to-cervera-de-pisuerga-circular-route-through-the-montana-palentina-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Reoba to Cervera de Pisuerga - circular route through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 20.4 km loop that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Dehesa de Montejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from tranquil river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to explore ancient woodlands and cultural sites. The well-maintained trails and scenic views are frequently highlighted as major attractions.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs to see while mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. The region features several beautiful lakes and reservoirs that can be incorporated into your mountain biking adventures. You can ride around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesga Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5595157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requejada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153477891/vuelta-de-la-embalsa-de-la-ruesga-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta de la Embalsa de la Ruesga — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that winds around the reservoir, offering scenic views.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with cultural exploration in Dehesa de Montejo?
A: Yes, Dehesa de Montejo is integrated into historical routes like the Natural Route of Romanesque Art in Palencia, allowing you to combine your ride with cultural exploration. You can pass through charming villages and discover points of interest such as the Church of San Pelayo, a Romanesque temple. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176620244/palencian-romanesque-natural-trail-coking-coal-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencian Romanesque Natural Trail: Coking Coal Route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that leads through historical landscapes.
tips: This old fountain is just off the main road. There are a few places to sit and a laundry area for old clothes here as well.

The hermitage of Santa Cecilia de Vallespinoso de Aguilar, declared a Historic-Artistic Monument, combines nature, landscape and art. Its construction in the 12th century presents a rectangular nave with a cannon cover and a semicircular apse. Its exterior stands out with well-worked ashlars, a peculiar tower and an imposing cover. The interior impresses with its verticality and a beautiful staircase that joins the nave and head. Sculptures such as the Samson capital and the acanthus leaf capital accentuate its beauty.
This is a great trail that goes through this valley surrounded by mountains and fields. It's great on a mountain bike or gravel bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete La Ruta de las Ermitas by mountain bike?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is approximately 49 kilometers long and generally takes around 4 hours and 24 minutes to complete. This duration is for continuous riding and does not include stops for breaks or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: La Ruta de las Ermitas is rated as a moderate mountain bike trail. While the Montaña Palentina region can offer very demanding multi-stage routes, this specific circular route is suitable for riders with a good level of fitness and some experience with varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, with approximately 70% tracks, 20% trails, and 10% asphalt. You'll navigate through dense forests of beech, oak, and pine, as well as along rural paths. Expect diverse landscapes, including some high mountain areas, offering a challenging and scenic ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you can discover several notable spots. Highlights include the Fuente de Perazancas, the historic Hermitage of Santa Cecilia, and a Scenic Dirt Road Through the Valley. The trail also offers views of prominent peaks like Curavacas and Espigüete, and passes through lush forests.

faqs.question: Is La Ruta de las Ermitas dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of abundant wildlife, including brown bears and large deer, and to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to bike in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for general recreational activities like mountain biking on established trails within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for any specific protected areas or seasonal restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike La Ruta de las Ermitas is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. During these periods, the weather is more favorable, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Winter can bring snow and ice, making some sections impassable or significantly more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for this specific Komoot tour can be found on the tour map, many circular routes in the Montaña Palentina region commonly start and end in towns like Cervera de Pisuerga, which offers parking facilities. We recommend checking the detailed Komoot map for the most precise trailhead access and parking options.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural Montaña Palentina is known for its rich biodiversity. Riders have the possibility of sighting brown bears and large deer, among other abundant wildlife. Maintaining a respectful distance and not disturbing animals is essential.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 2, the Románico Palentino, and the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 3. Other smaller sections include the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey) and the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina region, while wild, has towns like Cervera de Pisuerga that serve as hubs for visitors. These towns typically offer various services including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. It's advisable to plan your stops in advance, especially in less populated areas along the route.
